svg.aria-label: The four typed memories fed by a central write router. An incoming item enters the write router, which classifies it by the kind of knowledge it carries and sends it to one of four labeled stores. Working memory holds what we are doing now, a volatile scratchpad, example task_state quoting the refund for ORD-3300 now. Semantic memory holds what is true, durable facts, example user_profile name Dana prefers email. Episodic memory holds what happened, an event log that formalizes Part 5's reflection buffer, example user opened a return request for ORD-3300 earlier today. Procedural memory holds how to do a task, learned rules that hold Part 5's promoted reflection, example returns after the window incur a 10% fee, multiply the total by 0.9. Off to the side an OS style placement sketch maps the stores onto three access tiers, core always in context, recall recent and paged in, archival large and searched via a black-box vector search reusing RAG, not re-derived here.
svg.title: The four typed memories and the write router, with OS-style placement tiers

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: Four typed memories, by the KIND of knowledge they hold, fed by one write router

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: A flat buffer cannot tell what HAPPENED from what is TRUE from how to DO a task. The router classifies each item and routes it to the matching store.

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: Two axes, not one. The four KINDS (working, semantic, episodic, procedural) say what a memory IS; the three TIERS (core, recall, archival)

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, sans-serif

svg.text: say how it is REACHED. A write router places by kind; the access tiers decide what fits in context now versus what is searched on demand.
